lines.english: Dhanaasaree, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਹਰਿ ਸੇਵਹਿ ਤਿਨ ਬਲਿ ਜਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jo har seveh tin bal jaau |
lines.english: I am a sacrifice to those who serve the Lord.
O Nanak, the Naam is the Embellisher. ||4||4||
True is service to the True Lord God.
Through the True Word of the Shabad, the Lord comes to dwell in their minds. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਸਾਚੁ ਸਚਾ ਮੁਖਿ ਨਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: tin hiradai saach sachaa mukh naau |
lines.english: The Truth is in their hearts, and the True Name is on their lips.
Dwelling upon the Truest of the True, their pains are dispelled.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਹਜੇ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਮੰਨਿ ਵਸਾਏ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sahaje har naam man vasaae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: and they naturally enshrine the Lord's Name in their minds. ||1||Pause||
Listening to the Word of Gurbani, filth is washed off,
finds tranquility, peace and pleasure within.
One who conquers fraud, deceit and the fire of desire

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਭਾਣੈ ਚਲੈ ਤਾ ਆਪੁ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: gur kai bhaanai chalai taa aap jaae |
lines.english: If one walks in harmony with the Guru's Will, he eliminates his self-conceit.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਚੁ ਮਹਲੁ ਪਾਏ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: saach mehal paae har gun gaae |2|
lines.english: He finds the True Mansion of the Lord's Presence, singing the Glorious Praises of the Lord. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨ ਸਬਦੁ ਬੂਝੈ ਨ ਜਾਣੈ ਬਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: n sabad boojhai na jaanai baanee |
lines.english: The blind, self-willed manmukh does not understand the Shabad; he does not know the Word of the Guru's Bani,
and so he passes his life in misery.
But if he meets the True Guru, then he finds peace,
and the ego within is silenced. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਸ ਨੋ ਕਹੀਐ ਦਾਤਾ ਇਕੁ ਸੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: kis no kaheeai daataa ik soe |
lines.english: Who else should I speak to? The One Lord is the Giver of all.
O Nanak, becoming truthful, I have become pleasing to the True Lord. ||4||5||
When He grants His Grace, then we obtain the Word of the Shabad.
Meeting with my Beloved, I sing the Glorious Praises of the True Lord.
Dhanaasaree, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਮਰੈ ਦਾਰੂ ਜਾਣੈ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: eihu man marai daaroo jaanai koe |
lines.english: Rare is the one who knows the medicine to conquer the mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਮਨ ਮੂਏ ਕੈਸੇ ਹਰਿ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: bin man mooe kaise har paae |
lines.english: Without conquering the mind, how can the Lord be found?
When the mind is conquered, its turbulent wanderings are stopped.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨੁ ਸਬਦਿ ਮਰੈ ਬੂਝੈ ਜਨੁ ਸੋਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: man sabad marai boojhai jan soe |1|
lines.english: The mind is conquered through the Word of the Shabad; this is known to the Lord's humble servant. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਬਖਸੇ ਹਰਿ ਦੇ ਵਡਿਆਈ ॥
lines.translit: jis no bakhase har de vaddiaaee |
lines.english: The Lord forgives him, and blesses him with glory.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦਿ ਵਸੈ ਮਨਿ ਆਈ ॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: gur parasaad vasai man aaee | rahaau |
lines.english: By Guru's Grace, the Lord comes to dwell in the mind. ||Pause||
The Gurmukh does good deeds,

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਇਸੁ ਮਨ ਕੀ ਸੋਝੀ ਪਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: taa is man kee sojhee paavai |
lines.english: and so, he comes to understand this mind.
The mind is intoxicated, like the elephant with wine.
If someone eats the uneatable, then he becomes immaculate.
The mind is undisciplined; only a rare few can discipline it.
The Guru places the harness upon it, and rejuvenates it. ||2||
As Gurmukh, his mind is embellished.
Egotism and corruption are eradicated from within. ||3||
Those whom the Primal Lord keeps united in His Union,
shall never be separated from Him; they are merged in the Word of the Shabad.
Only God Himself knows His own power.
O Nanak, the Gurmukh realizes the Naam, the Name of the Lord. ||4||6||
The ignorant fools amass false wealth.
The blind, foolish, self-willed manmukhs have gone astray.
Dhanaasaree, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਖਿਆ ਕੈ ਧਨਿ ਸਦਾ ਦੁਖੁ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: bikhiaa kai dhan sadaa dukh hoe |
lines.english: Poisonous wealth brings constant pain.
True wealth is obtained through the Guru's Teachings.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਸਾਥਿ ਜਾਇ ਨ ਪਰਾਪਤਿ ਹੋਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naa saath jaae na paraapat hoe |1|
lines.english: It will not go with you, and it will not yield any profit. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਵਜਲਿ ਡੂਬੇ ਨ ਉਰਵਾਰਿ ਨ ਪਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: bhavajal ddoobe na uravaar na paar |
lines.english: They drown in the terrifying world-ocean, and they cannot reach either this shore, or the one beyond.
The foolish self-willed manmukhs all go astray and die.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਚਾ ਧਨੁ ਫੁਨਿ ਆਵੈ ਜਾਏ ॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: kaachaa dhan fun aavai jaae | rahaau |
lines.english: False wealth continues coming and going. ||Pause||
But by perfect destiny, they meet the True Guru;

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਹੁ ਜੁਗ ਮਹਿ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਸਾਚੀ ਬਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: chahu jug meh amrit saachee baanee |
lines.english: Throughout the four ages, the True Bani of His Word is Ambrosial Nectar.
imbued with the True Name, day and night, they remain detached from the world. ||2||
It is obtained only by perfect destiny. ||3||
By perfect destiny, one is absorbed in the True Name.
The Siddhas, the seekers and all men long for the Name.
Only a few realize the exalted Lord God.
He is the Truest of the True; He Himself implants the True Name within.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭੁ ਕਿਛੁ ਸਾਚਾ ਸਾਚਾ ਹੈ ਸੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: sabh kichh saachaa saachaa hai soe |
lines.english: The True Lord is everything; He is True.
